active_change: none (assess + analyze complete; not yet specced/planned)
phase_progress: ASSESS + ANALYZE COMPLETE. Root cause of the reported 'UAR cannot be found in PATH' cloud bug: a MISSING CAPABILITY, not a misconfiguration. LibreFang never builds, ships, spawns, or supervises a UAR process — Dockerfile:177-178 links UAR in-process and copies only the librefang binary; k8s/base/bossfang-deployment.yaml:64-68 has a single container; no spawn code exists. The error surfaces from the generic channel sidecar supervisor (sidecar.rs:783 Command::new, wrapper :822-827) when UAR is hand-configured as a [[sidecar_channels]] entry, because resolve_sidecar_command (sidecar.rs:728) resolves only the Telegram stem and UAR falls through to a bare PATH lookup for a binary that was never shipped. ANALYZE: UAR is NOT a greenfield integration — it already has a Dockerfile, a published image (us-docker.pkg.dev/.../universal-agent-runtime), a release pipeline, a live GKE Deployment (deploy/uar, svc uar-svc), a purpose-built `uar-sidecar` binary (READY:{port} stdout handshake + stdin-EOF shutdown), and a full HTTP control surface (/healthz, /readyz, /api/models, /api/chat/completion, SSE /api/live). Build-vs-adopt: ZERO new third-party deps — ADOPT UAR's image (COPY --from), REUSE librefang's own sidecar supervisor (sidecar.rs:502-520 already has spawn+restart+backoff) and its reqwest 0.13 (stream)/futures/tokio-stream stack. DECISIONS (user-confirmed): D-1 shape = child process in-container (rejected k8s native sidecar container — cannot start/stop a sibling container from inside the pod — and client-of-existing-Deployment — no lifecycle control); D-2 uar-driver = un-force now (drop the unconditional edge at librefang-kernel/Cargo.toml:16), delete later. KEY FINDING: cargo tree -i surrealdb proves UAR (=3.2.1, EXACT) is the SOLE rigid constraint; surreal-memory (3.2.0, CARET) flexes freely — so retiring the in-process link DOES remove the 3-repo lockstep pin. BLOCKING UPSTREAM CHANGE: GQAdonis/universal-agent-runtime does not build/publish `uar-sidecar` (release.yml has 0 refs; Dockerfile:225 builds only --bin universal-agent-runtime) — small additive change, blocks G-1. Artifacts: assessment.md, analysis.md, library-candidates.json, decision-log.md, docs/architecture/uar-runtime-integration-research.md. NOTE: all of this sits on an UNCOMMITTED upstream merge (68 commits) + surrealdb 3.2.1 retarget in worktree /tmp/librefang-upstream-merge.

next_action.description: Assess + analyze are complete with zero new third-party dependencies and two user-confirmed decisions. Remaining blockers: G-1 (UAR must publish the `uar-sidecar` binary — upstream), G-3 (READY handshake + stdin-EOF shutdown on the existing supervisor), G-4 (generalize resolve_sidecar_command so PATH is never load-bearing). Spec the acceptance criteria, then /kbd-plan. Consider landing the upstream UAR change (add --bin uar-sidecar) in parallel, since it gates end-to-end testing.
